comments.author: reader
comments.text: The Elm rule should be that if the incoming board data provides any gesture data, we honor it (insofar as it obeys geometry). But if the gesture data is not present, Elm just simulates it.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Not quite. If Python provides intra-board gesture paths, they are there for a reason! What would the point be for Python to compute gesture paths if Elm would only ignore them?

comments.author: reader
comments.text: I would have two closely related dimensions. When actions come the enum is LocallyOriginated vs. FromWire. And then Fathful-vs.-Simulated is an outcome that is only **partly** a consequence of Local vs. FromWire.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Again, don't put the decision in the wrong place here. Or I should don't make the decision at the wrong time. When you record the event, just record whether it comes from the original UI or the  wire. Simple. Defer the actual decision making about Faithful vs. Simulated to when it's actually relevant.
